sentences.sentence: More than a third of the world's soil is endangered, according to a recent UN report.
sentences.translation: 根据联合国最近的一份报告，世界上超过三分之一的土壤正处于危险之中。

structure.text: More than a third of the world's soil
according to a recent UN report

difficult_points: More than a third of the world's soil 是数量短语作主语，核心是 a third。
according to a recent UN report 是句末来源说明，不是句子的主要动作。
is endangered 使用被动语态，强调土壤处于受威胁的状态。

sentences.sentence: If we don't slow the decline, all farmable soil could be gone in 60 years.
sentences.translation: 如果我们不减缓这种衰退，所有可耕种土壤可能会在 60 年内消失。

structure.text: If we don't slow the decline

difficult_points: If 引导条件状语从句，说明后半句结果发生的前提。
could be gone 表示可能完全消失，could 体现预测的不确定性。
the decline 指上一句提到的土壤受威胁、退化的趋势。

sentences.sentence: Since soil grows 95% of our food, and sustains human life in other more surprising ways, that is a huge problem.
sentences.translation: 由于土壤生产了我们 95% 的食物，并且还以其他更令人意想不到的方式维系人类生命，这就是一个巨大的问题。

structure.text: Since soil grows 95% of our food, and sustains human life in other more surprising ways
and sustains human life in other more surprising ways

difficult_points: Since 在句首引导原因状语从句，表示“由于”，不是时间上的“自从”。
grows 和 sustains 是同一主语 soil 的两个并列谓语。
that 回指前一句所说可耕土壤可能消失这一情况。

expressions.meaning_zh: 以其他更令人意想不到的方式

sentences.sentence: Peter Groffman, from the Cary Institute of Ecosystem Studies in New York, points out that soil scientists have been warning about the degradation of the world's soil for decades.
sentences.translation: 纽约凯里生态系统研究所的 Peter Groffman 指出，土壤科学家几十年来一直在警告世界土壤正在退化。

structure.text: from the Cary Institute of Ecosystem Studies in New York
that soil scientists have been warning about the degradation of the world's soil for decades
have been warning about the degradation of the world's soil for decades

difficult_points: from the Cary Institute of Ecosystem Studies in New York 是插入说明 Peter Groffman 身份的介词短语。
that 引导 points out 的宾语从句，真正被指出的内容在 that 后。
have been warning 使用现在完成进行时，强调警告从过去持续到现在。

sentences.sentence: At the same time, our understanding of its importance to humans has grown.
sentences.translation: 与此同时，我们对土壤对人类重要性的理解也加深了。

structure.text: our understanding of its importance to humans

difficult_points: At the same time 承接上句，表示科学界一边警告退化，一边加深对土壤价值的认识。
its importance 中 its 指 soil，而不是上一句的人名或机构。
has grown 是现在完成时，表示理解程度已经发展并影响现在。

sentences.sentence: A single gram of healthy soil might contain 100 million bacteria, as well as other microorganisms such as viruses and fungi, living amid decomposing plants and various minerals.
sentences.translation: 一克健康土壤就可能含有一亿个细菌，以及病毒、真菌等其他微生物，它们生活在腐烂植物和各种矿物质之间。

structure.text: A single gram of healthy soil
as well as other microorganisms such as viruses and fungi
living amid decomposing plants and various minerals

difficult_points: A single gram 强调即使极少量健康土壤也含有大量生命。
as well as 连接 bacteria 与 other microorganisms，表示“以及”。
living amid ... 是现在分词短语，补充说明微生物所处的环境。

sentences.sentence: That means soils do not just grow our food, but are the source of nearly all our existing antibiotics, and could be our best hope in the fight against antibiotic-resistant bacteria.
sentences.translation: 这意味着土壤不只是种出我们的食物，还是我们几乎所有现有抗生素的来源，并且可能是我们对抗耐抗生素细菌的最大希望。

structure.text: soils do not just grow our food, but are the source of nearly all our existing antibiotics, and could be our best hope in the fight against antibiotic-resistant bacteria
do not just grow our food, but are the source of nearly all our existing antibiotics
and could be our best hope in the fight against antibiotic-resistant bacteria

difficult_points: That 回指前一句健康土壤中含有丰富微生物这一事实。
do not just ..., but ... 构成递进关系，强调土壤价值不止于生产食物。
could be our best hope 中 could 表示可能性，语气比直接判断更谨慎。

sentences.sentence: Soil is also an ally against climate change: as microorganisms within soil digest dead animals and plants, they lock in their carbon content, holding three times the amount of carbon as does the entire atmosphere.
sentences.translation: 土壤也是对抗气候变化的盟友：当土壤中的微生物分解死亡的动植物时，它们会锁住这些动植物的碳含量，储存的碳量是整个大气的三倍。

structure.text: Soil is also an ally against climate change
as microorganisms within soil digest dead animals and plants
they lock in their carbon content
holding three times the amount of carbon as does the entire atmosphere

difficult_points: 冒号后解释土壤为什么能成为对抗气候变化的盟友。
as 引导时间状语从句，表示微生物分解动植物的同时发生后面的锁碳过程。
holding ... 是现在分词短语，说明锁碳产生的结果或伴随状态。
as does the entire atmosphere 是比较结构的倒装，相当于 as the entire atmosphere does。

sentences.sentence: Soils also store water, preventing flood damage: in the UK, damage to buildings, roads and bridges from floods caused by soil degradation costs £233 million every year.
sentences.translation: 土壤还会储水，从而防止洪水损害：在英国，由土壤退化引发的洪水对建筑物、道路和桥梁造成的损失每年达 2.33 亿英镑。

structure.text: damage to buildings, roads and bridges from floods caused by soil degradation

difficult_points: preventing flood damage 是现在分词短语，说明 store water 带来的结果。
冒号后给出英国的具体经济损失例子来支撑前半句。
caused by soil degradation 是过去分词短语，修饰 floods，而不是 buildings, roads and bridges。

sentences.sentence: If the soil loses its ability to perform these functions, the human race could be in big trouble.
sentences.translation: 如果土壤失去履行这些功能的能力，人类可能会陷入大麻烦。

structure.text: If the soil loses its ability to perform these functions
loses its ability to perform these functions

difficult_points: If 引导条件状语从句，说明人类陷入麻烦的前提。
these functions 指前文提到的提供食物、抗生素、锁碳和储水等功能。
to perform these functions 是不定式短语，修饰 ability。

sentences.sentence: The danger is not that the soil will disappear completely, but that the microorganisms that give it its special properties will be lost.
sentences.translation: 危险并不在于土壤会完全消失，而在于赋予土壤特殊属性的微生物将会消失。

structure.text: is not that the soil will disappear completely, but that the microorganisms that give it its special properties will be lost
that the soil will disappear completely
that the microorganisms that give it its special properties will be lost
that give it its special properties

difficult_points: not ..., but ... 构成否定前者、强调后者的对比结构。
第二个 that 引导表语从句，说明真正的危险是什么。
that give it its special properties 是定语从句，修饰 microorganisms；it 指 soil。

sentences.sentence: And once this has happened, it may take the soil thousands of years to recover.
sentences.translation: 而一旦这种情况发生，土壤可能需要数千年才能恢复。

structure.text: it may take the soil thousands of years to recover

difficult_points: once 引导时间条件从句，表示“一旦”。
this 指上一句中土壤微生物丧失的情况。
it may take ... to recover 是固定句型，it 是形式主语。

sentences.sentence: Agriculture is by far the biggest problem.
sentences.translation: 农业无疑是最大的问题。

structure.text: is by far the biggest problem

difficult_points: by far 用来加强最高级 the biggest，表示“显然、远远地”。
the biggest problem 承接前文，说明土壤退化的主要原因转向农业活动。
句子虽短，但 Agriculture 在这里指人类农业生产整体，而非某一次耕作。

sentences.sentence: In the wild, when plants grow they remove nutrients from the soil, but then when the plants die and decay these nutrients are returned directly to the soil.
sentences.translation: 在野外，植物生长时会从土壤中吸收养分；但随后当植物死亡并腐烂时，这些养分又会直接回到土壤中。

structure.text: they remove nutrients from the soil
but then when the plants die and decay these nutrients are returned directly to the soil
when the plants die and decay

difficult_points: 两个 when 从句分别说明植物生长时和死亡腐烂时发生的相反过程。
they 指 plants，these nutrients 指前半句从土壤中移走的 nutrients。
are returned 使用被动语态，强调养分回到土壤，而不是强调执行者。

sentences.sentence: Humans tend not to return unused parts of harvested crops directly to the soil to enrich it, meaning that the soil gradually becomes less fertile.
sentences.translation: 人类往往不会把收获作物中未使用的部分直接还给土壤来增加其养分，这意味着土壤会逐渐变得不那么肥沃。

structure.text: tend not to return unused parts of harvested crops directly to the soil
meaning that the soil gradually becomes less fertile
that the soil gradually becomes less fertile

difficult_points: tend not to 表示“往往不”，描述一种普遍倾向而非绝对情况。
to enrich it 是目的状语，it 指 soil。
meaning that ... 是现在分词短语，说明前面做法造成的结果。

sentences.sentence: In the past we developed strategies to get around the problem, such as regularly varying the types of crops grown, or leaving fields uncultivated for a season.
sentences.translation: 过去，我们制定了一些策略来绕开这个问题，例如定期改变种植的作物类型，或让田地休耕一个季节。

structure.text: such as regularly varying the types of crops grown, or leaving fields uncultivated for a season

difficult_points: to get around the problem 是不定式短语，说明 developed strategies 的目的。
such as 后列举两种策略：改变作物类型和休耕。
grown 是过去分词，修饰 crops，表示“被种植的作物”。

sentences.sentence: But these practices became inconvenient as populations grew and agriculture had to be run on more commercial lines.
sentences.translation: 但随着人口增长、农业不得不以更商业化的方式运作，这些做法变得不方便了。

structure.text: as populations grew and agriculture had to be run on more commercial lines
agriculture had to be run on more commercial lines

difficult_points: these practices 指上一段提到的轮作和休耕等传统做法。
as 在这里表示“随着/由于”，引出背景变化。
had to be run 使用被动语态，说明农业经营方式被迫改变。

sentences.sentence: A solution came in the early 20th century with the Haber-Bosch process for manufacturing ammonium nitrate.
sentences.translation: 20 世纪初，随着用于制造硝酸铵的哈伯—博施法出现，一个解决办法也随之而来。
difficult_points: with the Haber-Bosch process 表示解决办法伴随这一工艺出现。
for manufacturing ammonium nitrate 修饰 process，说明该工艺的用途。
came 在这里不是“来到某地”，而是“出现”。

sentences.sentence: Farmers have been putting this synthetic fertiliser on their fields ever since.
sentences.translation: 从那以后，农民们就一直把这种合成肥料施到田地里。

structure.text: have been putting this synthetic fertiliser on their fields

difficult_points: have been putting 是现在完成进行时，强调从过去开始持续施用到现在。
this synthetic fertiliser 指上一句中由哈伯—博施法制造的硝酸铵肥料。
ever since 表示“自那以后一直”，承接 early 20th century。

sentences.sentence: But over the past few decades, it has become clear this wasn't such a bright idea.
sentences.translation: 但在过去几十年里，人们已经清楚地意识到，这并不是一个多么明智的主意。

structure.text: this wasn't such a bright idea

difficult_points: it has become clear 中 it 是形式主语，真正内容是后面的 this wasn't such a bright idea。
this 指长期施用合成化肥这一做法。
bright idea 在这里是口语化表达，意为“明智的主意”，句中带否定意味。

sentences.sentence: Chemical fertilisers can release polluting nitrous oxide into the atmosphere and excess is often washed away with the rain, releasing nitrogen into rivers.
sentences.translation: 化学肥料会向大气中释放造成污染的一氧化二氮，过量的肥料又常被雨水冲走，把氮释放进河流。

structure.text: can release polluting nitrous oxide into the atmosphere
and excess is often washed away with the rain

difficult_points: and 连接两个并列分句：化肥释放气体，以及过量部分被雨水冲走。
excess 在这里作名词，指过量的化肥。
releasing nitrogen into rivers 是现在分词短语，说明被雨水冲走后的结果。

sentences.sentence: More recently, we have found that indiscriminate use of fertilisers hurts the soil itself, turning it acidic and salty, and degrading the soil they are supposed to nourish.
sentences.translation: 更近一些时候，我们发现，不加区分地使用肥料会伤害土壤本身，使其变酸、变咸，并使这些本应被滋养的土壤退化。

structure.text: that indiscriminate use of fertilisers hurts the soil itself, turning it acidic and salty, and degrading the soil they are supposed to nourish

difficult_points: that 引导 have found 的宾语从句，说明最新发现的内容。
turning 和 degrading 是两个并列的现在分词短语，说明 fertilisers 伤害土壤的具体方式。
they are supposed to nourish 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 soil；they 指 fertilisers。

sentences.sentence: One of the people looking for a solution to this problem is Pius Floris, who started out running a tree-care business in the Netherlands, and now advises some of the world's top soil scientists.
sentences.translation: 寻找这一问题解决方案的人之一是 Pius Floris，他最初在荷兰经营树木护理业务，如今为一些世界顶尖土壤科学家提供建议。

structure.text: One of the people looking for a solution to this problem
looking for a solution to this problem
who started out running a tree-care business in the Netherlands, and now advises some of the world's top soil scientists
started out running a tree-care business in the Netherlands

difficult_points: looking for a solution to this problem 是现在分词短语，修饰 people。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充介绍 Pius Floris 的背景和现状。
started out running ... and now advises ... 形成过去起点与现在工作的对比。

sentences.sentence: He came to realise that the best way to ensure his trees flourished was to take care of the soil, and has developed a cocktail of beneficial bacteria, fungi and humus* to do this.
sentences.translation: 他逐渐意识到，确保树木茁壮生长的最佳方式是照料好土壤，并为此开发出一种由有益细菌、真菌和腐殖质组成的混合物。

structure.text: that the best way to ensure his trees flourished was to take care of the soil
and has developed a cocktail of beneficial bacteria, fungi and humus* to do this

difficult_points: came to realise 表示逐渐意识到，而不是突然知道。
that 引导 realise 的宾语从句，其中 the best way 是从句主语。
and has developed 与 came to realise 并列，主语同为 He。
to do this 中 this 指照料土壤、确保树木茁壮。

sentences.sentence: Researchers at the University of Valladolid in Spain recently used this cocktail on soils destroyed by years of fertiliser overuse.
sentences.translation: 西班牙巴利亚多利德大学的研究人员最近把这种混合物用在多年过度施肥而受损的土壤上。

structure.text: Researchers at the University of Valladolid in Spain
recently used this cocktail on soils
destroyed by years of fertiliser overuse

difficult_points: at the University of Valladolid in Spain 修饰 Researchers，说明研究者所属机构。
this cocktail 指上一句 Floris 开发的有益细菌、真菌和腐殖质混合物。
destroyed by years of fertiliser overuse 是过去分词短语，修饰 soils。

sentences.sentence: When they applied Floris's mix to the desert-like test plots, a good crop of plants emerged that were not just healthy at the surface, but had roots strong enough to pierce dirt as hard as rock.
sentences.translation: 当他们把 Floris 的混合物施用到类似沙漠的试验田时，一批长势良好的植物出现了；这些植物不仅表面健康，而且根部强壮到足以穿透像岩石一样坚硬的泥土。

structure.text: When they applied Floris's mix to the desert-like test plots
that were not just healthy at the surface, but had roots strong enough to pierce dirt as hard as rock
strong enough to pierce dirt as hard as rock

difficult_points: When 引导时间状语从句，说明植物出现的实验条件。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 plants，而不是 crop。
not just ..., but ... 表示递进，强调植物不仅表面健康，根也很强壮。
as hard as rock 是比较结构，形容 dirt 的坚硬程度。

sentences.sentence: The few plants that grew in the control plots, fed with traditional fertilisers, were small and weak.
sentences.translation: 在使用传统肥料的对照地块中长出的少数植物则又小又弱。

structure.text: The few plants that grew in the control plots
that grew in the control plots

difficult_points: The few plants 强调对照地块中长出的植物数量很少。
that grew in the control plots 是定语从句，限定 plants。
fed with traditional fertilisers 是过去分词短语，补充说明这些对照地块植物的处理方式。

sentences.sentence: However, measures like this are not enough to solve the global soil degradation problem.
sentences.translation: 然而，像这样的措施还不足以解决全球土壤退化问题。

structure.text: to solve the global soil degradation problem

difficult_points: However 表示转折，说明前段的土壤改良方法仍有局限。
measures like this 指 Floris 的混合物这类局部改良措施。
to solve ... 是不定式短语，说明 enough 的判定标准。

sentences.sentence: To assess our options on a global scale we first need an accurate picture of what types of soil are out there, and the problems they face.
sentences.translation: 为了在全球范围内评估我们的选择，我们首先需要准确了解外界有哪些土壤类型，以及它们面临哪些问题。

structure.text: To assess our options on a global scale
of what types of soil are out there, and the problems they face
what types of soil are out there

difficult_points: To assess our options on a global scale 是目的状语，说明为什么需要准确图景。
an accurate picture of 后接两个并列内容：土壤类型和它们面临的问题。
what types of soil are out there 是嵌入式疑问结构，作 of 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: That's not easy.
sentences.translation: 这并不容易。
difficult_points: That 指上一句所说在全球范围内获得准确土壤图景这件事。
not easy 是简短评价，引出下一句解释困难所在。
句子虽短，但在段落结构上起到承上启下的作用。

sentences.sentence: For one thing, there is no agreed international system for classifying soil.
sentences.translation: 首先，并不存在一个得到一致认可的国际土壤分类体系。

structure.text: there is no agreed international system

difficult_points: For one thing 用来引出原因之一，解释上一句为什么 not easy。
there is no ... 是存在句，强调缺少统一体系。
agreed 作形容词，表示“各方认可的、一致同意的”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 得到一致认可的国际体系

sentences.sentence: In an attempt to unify the different approaches, the UN has created the Global Soil Map project.
sentences.translation: 为了尝试统一不同的方法，联合国创建了“全球土壤地图”项目。

structure.text: In an attempt to unify the different approaches
has created the Global Soil Map project

difficult_points: In an attempt to ... 表示目的，相当于“为了尝试……”。
the different approaches 指各国或各研究体系对土壤分类和测量的不同方法。
has created 使用现在完成时，强调项目已经建立并与当前工作相关。

sentences.sentence: Researchers from nine countries are working together to create a map linked to a database that can be fed measurements from field surveys, drone surveys, satellite imagery, lab analyses and so on to provide real-time data on the state of the soil.
sentences.translation: 来自九个国家的研究人员正在合作创建一张连接数据库的地图，该数据库可以接收来自实地调查、无人机调查、卫星图像、实验室分析等的测量数据，以提供土壤状态的实时数据。

structure.text: are working together to create a map
that can be fed measurements from field surveys, drone surveys, satellite imagery, lab analyses and so on
to provide real-time data on the state of the soil

difficult_points: are working together to create ... 表示正在合作完成的目标。
linked to a database 是过去分词短语，修饰 map。
that can be fed measurements ... 是定语从句，修饰 database，说明数据库如何获取数据。
to provide real-time data ... 表示整个数据收集系统的目的。

sentences.sentence: Within the next four years, they aim to have mapped soils worldwide to a depth of 100 metres, with the results freely accessible to all.
sentences.translation: 他们的目标是在未来四年内完成对全球土壤深至 100 米的测绘，并让所有人都能免费获取结果。

structure.text: aim to have mapped soils worldwide to a depth of 100 metres
with the results freely accessible to all

difficult_points: Within the next four years 表示完成目标的时间范围。
aim to have mapped 使用完成式不定式，强调到目标时间点时测绘已经完成。
with the results freely accessible to all 是 with 复合结构，说明结果公开可得。

sentences.sentence: But this is only a first step.
sentences.translation: 但这只是第一步。
difficult_points: But 表示转折，说明全球土壤地图项目仍不足够。
this 指上一段的全球土壤测绘工作。
only a first step 强调该措施只是起点，还需要进一步行动。

sentences.sentence: We need ways of presenting the problem that bring it home to governments and the wider public, says Pamela Chasek at the International Institute for Sustainable Development, in Winnipeg, Canada.
sentences.translation: 加拿大温尼伯国际可持续发展研究所的 Pamela Chasek 说，我们需要一些呈现这一问题的方式，使政府和更广泛的公众真正意识到它。

structure.text: We need ways of presenting the problem
that bring it home to governments and the wider public
at the International Institute for Sustainable Development, in Winnipeg, Canada

difficult_points: says Pamela Chasek 是插入的转述语，主句内容是 We need ways ...。
that bring it home ... 是定语从句，修饰 ways。
it 指 the problem，bring it home to 表示让人真正理解其严重性。

sentences.sentence: 'Most scientists don't speak language that policy-makers can understand, and vice versa.'
sentences.translation: “大多数科学家不会说政策制定者能理解的语言，反之亦然。”
difficult_points: that policy-makers can understand 是定语从句，修饰 language。
vice versa 表示反过来也一样，即政策制定者也未必能用科学家理解的语言沟通。
don't speak language 这里不是指具体某种语言，而是指沟通方式和表达体系。

sentences.sentence: Chasek and her colleagues have proposed a goal of 'zero net land degradation'.
sentences.translation: Chasek 和她的同事提出了“土地净退化为零”的目标。
difficult_points: have proposed 使用现在完成时，说明该目标已经被提出并影响当前讨论。
a goal of 后面的引号内容具体说明目标是什么。
zero net land degradation 类似“净零”概念，强调总体退化量抵消到零。

sentences.sentence: Like the idea of carbon neutrality, it is an easily understood target that can help shape expectations and encourage action.
sentences.translation: 像碳中和这一概念一样，它是一个容易理解的目标，可以帮助塑造预期并鼓励行动。

structure.text: Like the idea of carbon neutrality
that can help shape expectations and encourage action

difficult_points: Like the idea of carbon neutrality 是比较参照，说明该目标的传播方式类似碳中和。
it 指上一句的 zero net land degradation 目标。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 target，说明这个目标的作用。

sentences.sentence: For soils on the brink, that may be too late.
sentences.translation: 对于濒临危险的土壤来说，那可能已经太迟了。
difficult_points: For soils on the brink 限定讨论对象，指已经处于危险边缘的土壤。
that 指前文提出目标、塑造预期和鼓励行动这一路径。
may be too late 表示这种长期目标对最危险土壤可能来不及。

sentences.sentence: Several researchers are agitating for the immediate creation of protected zones for endangered soils.
sentences.translation: 几位研究人员正在积极呼吁立即为濒危土壤建立保护区。

structure.text: are agitating for the immediate creation of protected zones
the immediate creation of protected zones

difficult_points: are agitating for 表示积极倡议或呼吁，不是简单的“烦躁”。
immediate creation 强调行动的紧迫性。
for endangered soils 说明保护区针对的是濒危土壤，而非所有土地。

sentences.sentence: One difficulty here is defining what these areas should conserve: areas where the greatest soil diversity is present?
sentences.translation: 这里的一个困难在于界定这些地区应当保护什么：是土壤多样性最丰富的地区吗？

structure.text: is defining what these areas should conserve
areas where the greatest soil diversity is present
where the greatest soil diversity is present

difficult_points: defining what ... 是动名词短语作表语，说明困难的具体内容。
what these areas should conserve 是嵌入式疑问结构，作 defining 的宾语。
冒号后提出第一个可能答案，以问句形式呈现保护对象的选择难题。

expressions.meaning_zh: 这些地区应当保护什么

sentences.sentence: Or areas of unspoilt soils that could act as a future benchmark of quality?
sentences.translation: 还是那些未被破坏、将来可以作为质量基准的土壤地区？

structure.text: that could act as a future benchmark of quality
act as a future benchmark of quality

difficult_points: Or 承接上一句冒号后的第一个问题，提出另一种保护对象。
that could act as ... 是定语从句，修饰 areas of unspoilt soils。
act as a future benchmark of quality 表示这些未受破坏的土壤可作为未来评价标准。

sentences.sentence: Whatever we do, if we want our soils to survive, we need to take action now.
sentences.translation: 无论我们做什么，如果想让土壤存续下去，我们都需要现在就采取行动。

structure.text: if we want our soils to survive

difficult_points: Whatever 引导让步从句，表示“不管采取哪种方式”。
if 引导条件状语从句，说明立即行动的前提目标是让土壤存续。
need to take action now 是文章结论，now 强调行动的紧迫性。
